name: Midwest Apple Crunch Pie
recipeIngredient: ½ cup Brown sugar, firmly packed
recipeInstructions:
1. If making your own crust, add the 2 tablespoons finely crushed granola to the flour before rolling out.
2. Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C).
3. For filling: Arrange apples in the unbaked pie shell.
4. Melt ¼ cup margarine.
5. Beat eggs in a medium bowl.
6. Add sweetened condensed milk, melted margarine, maple flavoring, cinnamon, and nutmeg; mix well.
7. Pour over apples.
8. For topping: Combine sugar and flour in a medium bowl. Cut in the remaining margarine until crumbly. Stir in finely crushed granola.
9. Sprinkle over custard.
10. Bake at 425°F (220°C) for 10 minutes. Reduce oven temperature to 375°F (190°C).
11. Continue baking at 375°F (190°C) for 35 to 40 minutes, or until golden brown. If necessary, cover the top or edge of the pie with foil to prevent overbrowning.
12. Serve warm, at room temperature, or chilled.
